reason: Native TacticalSolver at pinned settings (window_half=None, cand_cap=40, neighbor_dist=2) cannot compute T_provable within any feasible per-probe wall at either pre-registered rung. Both the primary rung (d9/2M) and the sole fallback rung (d7/1M) exceed the 8s/probe threshold by 3–75x AND return UNKNOWN (result=0, budget-exhausted) on every non-terminal turn-start. Head-lost proofs occur ONLY at the terminal-adjacent opponent-to-move ply (result=1, opponent completes the win next turn), which pins T_provable to game-end and manufactures a spurious V-KILL by construction (§6.1 red-team risk #1). This is the D-FORENSIC F2 / D-TACTICAL algorithm-bound failure: the tactics perf body (scored α-β / 729-eval / aged TT) is UNLANDED (memory d-zvalid-build-state), so neighbor_dist=2 candidate-gen exhausts budgets without proving.

shortest_loss_game_17ply_d9_300k.note: all non-terminal positions exhaust 300k budget → UNKNOWN

pilot_game0_185ply_d9_500k.note: dense late-game board → ~130s/probe even at reduced 500k budget; ~500-600s expected at spec 2M budget

proof_pattern: Across game0 (17-ply), game1 (19-ply), game2 (27-ply) losses: head_lost=True ONLY at the last turn-start (opponent-to-move, result=1, 2-6 nodes, instant). EVERY earlier turn-start returns result=0 (UNKNOWN) after exhausting the node budget. T_provable therefore always lands at the terminal-adjacent ply → lag_raw ≈ 0 for all games → ~100% EARLY/UNMEASURABLE.
consistency_with_prior: Matches D-FORENSIC F2 (memory d-forensic-f2-solver-algorithm-bound: 0/40 traps provable at 20k→3M nodes) and measure_native_provable_fraction.py (DEPTH=16 NODE_BUDGET=20000 on PRE-MINED near-mate traps). WP1 probes ARBITRARY mid-game turn-starts, a far harder proof target than mined traps, so the solver's inability to prove is expected and pre-registered as §6.1 risk #1.
budget_is_not_the_lever: Decisive control: at d9/20000 (the reference measure_native_provable_fraction.py budget) the solver runs FAST (median ~1.0-1.3s/probe, UNDER the 8s threshold) on 3 short loss games — but proves head_lost at ONLY the terminal-adjacent ply in every game (g0 ply15/17, g1 ply17/19, g2 ply25/27), IDENTICAL to the 2M-budget proof pattern. The large 2M/1M budgets only make probes SLOW (12-145s) WITHOUT proving anything more than the fast 20k budget does. This proves the ceiling is algorithm-bound (candidate-gen truncation, no scored α-β / aged TT), not budget-bound. No feasible rung — fast or slow — yields a T_provable earlier than terminal-adjacent, so lag_raw is uncomputable as a non-degenerate quantity at ANY budget within the frozen candidate-gen config.
verdict_contamination: Because T_provable pins to the terminal-adjacent ply for ~100% of games, lag_raw ≈ 0 universally → ~100% EARLY by §4.5 → a spurious V-KILL. §6.1 risk #1 states horizon truncation 'can manufacture V-KILL, it cannot manufacture V-CONFIRM' and that a V-KILL read under these conditions is NOT robust and must not be reported as a verdict. Producing any 248k verdict here would be a false V-KILL by construction. Hence ABORT rather than partial verdict, per dispatcher instruction and §5.6.
